_readme: Single source of truth for every non-standard/typo/shorthand glyph-name token the akhn generator has to special-case, so the mapping lives in data instead of scattered Python dict literals. Consumed by generate_akhn_feature.py's split_name(). Standard names are always checked FIRST in split_name() (against CONSONANTS/VOWEL_SIGN_ROOTS), so once a glyph listed here is manually renamed in the font to its standard/correct form, it resolves via that standard-name path and the corresponding entry below simply goes unused - no code change needed. Entries marked forward_compat exist purely to cover a rename that hasn't happened yet, so the code doesn't need editing again once it does.

raMatra.reason: This font's alternate token for consonant RA in its final/combining form (e.g. ba_ra-tutg is built from ba-tutg + raMatra-tutg components) - semantically just consonant RA for GSUB input-sequence purposes. Confirmed 2026-07-16: sa_ta_raMatra-tutg is the SA+TA+RA conjunct ("stra"), not an unparseable oddity.

vocalicR.reason: Different spelling/word-order for rVocalicMatra (sa_ka_vocalicR-tutg, ssa_ka_vocalicR-tutg). Confirmed 2026-07-16.

chillu_abbreviations._readme: "XChillu" is this font's shorthand for [consonant X] + Looped Virama (e.g. kChillu-tutg = ka-tutg + loopedvirama-tutg). Keys are the abbreviation prefix stripped of the trailing "Chillu"; values are the bare consonant name (no -tutg suffix - that's added by generate_akhn_feature.py).

ll.reason: MANUAL OVERRIDE, confirmed 2026-07-17 by project owner via visual inspection: despite the name implying LLA, llChillu-tutg is actually GA + Looped Virama. Can't be verified from the glif file the way ta_taChillu-tutg was (custom-drawn contours, no consonant-specific sub-part component) - taken on the project owner's word. GA *is* one of the proposal's documented Looped-Virama bases (K/G/TT/T/N), so this is consistent with that list.

rr.reason: MANUAL OVERRIDE, confirmed 2026-07-17 by project owner via visual inspection: despite the name implying RRA, rrChillu-tutg is actually RA + Looped Virama. Same caveat as "ll" above - not verifiable from glif components, taken on the project owner's word. RA is NOT on the proposal's documented Looped-Virama base list (K/G/TT/T/N), so this extends beyond the documented set rather than confirming it.

g.reason: llChillu-tutg is confirmed to actually be GA + Looped Virama (see "ll" above) - if it's ever manually renamed to gChillu-tutg to match its real sound (consistent with this font's single-letter-abbreviation convention for k/t/n/bh), this entry lets the renamed glyph keep resolving without touching the code.

r.reason: rrChillu-tutg is confirmed to actually be RA + Looped Virama (see "rr" above) - if it's ever manually renamed to rChillu-tutg to match its real sound, this entry lets the renamed glyph keep resolving without touching the code.

ta.reason: NOT the same thing as the standalone taChillu-tutg (see gsub_ignore_list.json - that one is a confirmed name/artwork mismatch, built from ya-tutg.below). This key only fires within a COMPOUND name like ta_taChillu-tutg, confirmed 2026-07-16 to be real T.T + Looped Virama (components: _taleftarm/_tamiddle - TA's own parts, same ones tChillu-tutg uses - plus _loopedviramaCombining.alt1 and a second _tamiddle). The gsub_ignore_list.json check in generate_akhn_feature.py's main loop is what guarantees this entry can never accidentally resolve the standalone broken taChillu-tutg too.

ligature_alt_overrides._readme: Per-glyph override for a ligature '.altN' glyph whose alt-ness is really just ONE component's own registered alt substituted in, confirmed per entry (not assumed from the name) - by visual inspection, or by the glyph's own component structure. 'resolved_target' names which resolved part (its own base glyph name, e.g. 'bha-tutg') gets swapped for its registered alt form, looked up in variant_registry.json using 'alt_index'; if that name appears more than once in the ligature's sequence, 'occurrence' (1-indexed, default 1) picks which one. Consumed by generate_akhn_feature.py's apply_ligature_alt_override(): the glyph keeps its real font name untouched (no UFO rename) but gets its own genuine TutgAkhand rule, keyed on the target's alt form, so it forms directly via TutgVariantSelect + TutgAkhand - no TutgConjunctVariantSelect/VS11 involved. generate_variant_registry.py excludes any glyph listed here from '_ligature_variants' accordingly, since it's handled by this mechanism instead.

bha_ra-tutg.alt1.reason: Confirmed 2026-07-21 by project owner via visual inspection: this glyph is bha's own alt1 form joined with plain ra, not an independent whole-ligature redesign.

ka_lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt.reason: Confirmed 2026-07-21 via the glif itself: ka_lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt is built from components ka-tutg + lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt (xOffset 5 / 1088) - the alt belongs to lVocalicMatra, not ka (ka-tutg has no registered alt of its own).

bha_lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt.reason: Confirmed 2026-07-21 via the glif itself: built from components bha-tutg + lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt, same pattern as ka_lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt.

da_lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt.reason: Confirmed 2026-07-21 via the glif itself: the vowel-sign part is a literal lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt component (da itself is drawn as a contour here rather than a da-tutg component, but that doesn't change which part the alt belongs to).

ba_lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt.reason: Same '<cons>_lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt' naming convention as the confirmed group above, per project owner 2026-07-21 - treated the same despite the vowel-sign part being drawn via a lVocalicMatra_tall-tutg.alt2 component instead of the plain lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt (a width-adjusted redraw of the same alt, not a different alt).

jha_lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt.reason: MANUAL OVERRIDE, project owner directive 2026-07-21: treat the same as the rest of the '<cons>_lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt' family despite no component evidence either way (the vowel-sign part is drawn as raw contours, not a component reference to lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt or lVocalicMatra_tall-tutg.alt2 - the jha part is a plain jha-tutg component, not one of jha's own registered alts, so by elimination the alt-ness isn't jha's).

lla_lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt.reason: MANUAL OVERRIDE, project owner directive 2026-07-21: treat the same as the rest of the '<cons>_lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt' family despite no component evidence either way (the vowel-sign part is drawn as raw contours, not a component reference to lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt or lVocalicMatra_tall-tutg.alt2). The lla part is drawn via lla-tutg.alt1 as its own component, but the ligature's own name still resolves the plain 'lla' token, so this override only ever swaps the lVocalicMatra part.

sa_lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt.reason: MANUAL OVERRIDE, project owner directive 2026-07-21: treat the same as the rest of the '<cons>_lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt' family despite no component evidence either way (the vowel-sign part is drawn as raw contours, not a component reference to lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt or lVocalicMatra_tall-tutg.alt2). sa-tutg has no registered alt of its own at all, so there's no competing claim on the alt-ness either.

ya_lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt.reason: MANUAL OVERRIDE, project owner directive 2026-07-21: treat the same as the rest of the '<cons>_lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt' family despite no component evidence either way (the vowel-sign part is drawn as raw contours, not a component reference to lVocalicMatra-tutg.alt or lVocalicMatra_tall-tutg.alt2). ya-tutg has no registered alt of its own at all, so there's no competing claim on the alt-ness either.

single_glyph_alt_overrides._readme: Per-glyph override for a single-glyph '.altN' sibling whose name doesn't match generate_variant_registry.py's ALT_RE ('<base>-tutg.altN') at all - so it's silently skipped by the regex, not even reported as unclassified/no-base. 'base' is the real base glyph this is an alt OF; 'index' is the selector index (VS1 = "1", etc.) it should register under, same as every other single-glyph variant_registry.json entry. Consumed directly by generate_variant_registry.py alongside its regex scan.

rVocalicMatra-tutg-alt1.reason: Uses a hyphen before "alt1" instead of the standard dot suffix (would be rVocalicMatra-tutg.alt1) - a naming inconsistency, confirmed 2026-07-21. This is rVocalicMatra-tutg's (Vocalic R vowel sign) own alt1 form, same role as e.g. uMatra-tutg's registered alts.
